title: Test thumbnails command
description: Write tests in tests/test_thumbnails.py covering the full pipeline (mocked subprocesses), multi-file splitting boundary conditions, hidden slide placeholder insertion, --plain output format, and all three missing-dependency error paths.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify a small deck produces JSON output listing thumbnails.jpg
Tests verify --cols and --output options propagate correctly
Tests verify multi-file output when slide count equals and exceeds cols × (cols + 1)
Tests verify hidden slide positions contain placeholder images rather than real renders
Tests verify missing Pillow produces correct stderr message and non-zero exit
Tests verify missing soffice produces correct stderr message and non-zero exit
Tests verify missing pdftoppm produces correct stderr message and non-zero exit

handoff_summary.completed: 38 tests pass across TestCheckDependencies (4), TestPptxToJpegs (5), TestMakeHatchedPlaceholder (3), TestAssembleGrid (6), TestRenderSlideImages (6), TestGenerateThumbnails (6), TestThumbnailsCmd (8). All acceptance criteria covered.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: thumbnails.py was modified (1 line bug fix). Any sibling bead also touching thumbnails.py should rebase.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Mocked subprocess.run and shutil.which throughout; used patch.dict(sys.modules, {'PIL': None}) to simulate missing Pillow. Production bug fix (slide.show → slide._element.get) was necessary to enable testing and matches the established codebase pattern.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: All acceptance criteria covered. Edge cases include: soffice/pdftoppm failure, missing PDF after soffice success, LibreOffice include-all vs skip-hidden hidden-slide modes, exact chunk boundary (cols*(cols+1)) vs overflow (cols*(cols+1)+1), --cols 7 rejection, --output prefix propagation, and all three dependency error paths.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Tests use pytest.importorskip("PIL") at module level — all 38 tests skip if Pillow is not installed. This is intentional since the module requires Pillow.

conflict_risks: thumbnails.py was modified (1 line bug fix). Any sibling bead also touching thumbnails.py should rebase.

execution_history.summary: Wrote tests/test_thumbnails.py with 38 tests covering all acceptance criteria. Also applied a 1-line production bug fix to pypptx/ops/thumbnails.py: slide.show does not exist in this python-pptx version; replaced with slide._element.get("show") in ("0", "false") matching the pattern already used in slides.py. All 38 new tests pass; 121 pre-existing tests unaffected.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Use `pytest` to target individual test files. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always use `pytest` for targeted runs. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is available before the structured verdict is submitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
